metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC3's proving command to also check `ast.Import` nodes for disallowed rebar bare-imports, e.g. add `for n in ast.walk(ast.parse(src)) if isinstance(n, ast.Import) for alias in n.names if alias.name.startswith('rebar') and not alias.name.startswith('rebar.llm.review_kernel')` to the `bad` list.
evidence: "bad=[m for n in ast.walk(ast.parse(src)) if isinstance(n,ast.ImportFrom) for m in [n.module or ''] if m.startswith('rebar') and not m.startswith('rebar.llm.review_kernel')]"
The check uses `isinstance(n, ast.ImportFrom)` exclusively; `ast.Import` (plain `import rebar.x`) is not covered — VERIFY-COMMAND DEFECT taxonomy item 4 (fixture does not fully exercise the claim).
findings.finding: AC3's proving command only walks `ast.ImportFrom` nodes to detect disallowed rebar imports, silently missing bare `import rebar.something` statements (`ast.Import` nodes). An accidental `import rebar.llm.plan_review.passes` in coach_moves.py would not be caught.
scenarios: A developer adds `import rebar.llm.plan_review.passes` as a bare import to coach_moves.py; the AC3 check passes green but a back-reference cycle has been introduced.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC no-back-reference check to also reject bare `ast.Import` nodes whose module names start with `rebar` but not `rebar.llm.review_kernel`, so all import forms (both `from X import Y` and `import X`) are covered.
evidence: The AC script: `isinstance(n, ast.ImportFrom)` — only catches `from X import Y` forms, not `import X.Y` bare imports (ast.Import nodes).
Plan states: 'It holds NO back-reference to passes.py, so no import cycle is introduced' — the intent covers all import forms.
The intended coach_moves.py implementation won't have bare imports to rebar.llm.passes, but the CI gate won't catch it if it did, leaving the no-cycle guarantee unverified by the stated check.
findings.finding: The AC no-back-reference verification script checks only `ast.ImportFrom` nodes (`isinstance(n, ast.ImportFrom)`), so a bare `import rebar.llm.passes` statement (an `ast.Import` node) in coach_moves.py would pass the AC gate undetected — silently permitting a back-reference that the plan explicitly prohibits.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC line 3: `python -c "import ast; src=open(...).read(); bad=[m for n in ast.walk(ast.parse(src)) if isinstance(n,ast.ImportFrom) for m in [n.module or ''] ...]"`
scenarios: An implementer accidentally writes `import rebar.llm.plan_review.passes` (a bare import) in coach_moves.py to resolve a name; the AC script reports no violations and CI goes green, but a real import cycle is introduced.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether docs/architecture.md has a module catalog section (separate from the offenders table) and, if so, add a `coach_moves.py` entry describing it as the deterministic Pass-4 move-catalog leaf.
evidence: Plan says: 'This ticket deletes that stale row' (the offenders-table row for passes.py)
AC only verifies the deletion of 'plan_review/passes.py' from architecture.md — no AC verifies that coach_moves.py is added to architecture.md's module listing if one exists
A new named sibling module added to a package is a documentable artifact that may need to appear in architecture.md's module catalog beyond the offenders table
findings.finding: The plan deletes the passes.py offenders-table row from docs/architecture.md but does not confirm whether coach_moves.py should be added to any normal module catalog or listing section in architecture.md. If architecture.md contains a module inventory (separate from the offenders table), the new leaf module would be absent from it — a stale-by-omission reference gap.
findings.location: Section 'Deviation from the docs/architecture.md offenders row' + AC line: `! grep -q 'plan_review/passes.py' docs/architecture.md`
scenarios: A developer consulting architecture.md to understand the plan_review package structure finds coach_moves.py undocumented in the module catalog while the offenders row (which formerly named it as a target) has been deleted

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before merging, update `docs/architecture.md` line ~271's offenders row to reflect the ACTUAL seam (move `MOVE_REGISTRY`, `load_move_registry`, `triage_advisories`, `APPLY_NOW_MARGIN`; `pass4_coach` stays due to circular import) in a separate prior or same-PR parent-spec correction, so the leaf is reviewable as a consistent subset of the corrected parent rather than a deletion of the parent spec.
evidence: Parent (docs/architecture.md line 271): 'Extract the coach cluster (`MOVE_REGISTRY`, `load_move_registry`, `pass4_coach`) into a sibling `coach_moves.py`'

Leaf plan 'Deviation' section: '`pass4_coach` CANNOT move — it builds a RunRequest through passes.py internals... so relocating it would create a circular import (leaf -> passes). So `pass4_coach` STAYS in passes.py, and `triage_advisories` + `APPLY_NOW_MARGIN`... are moved instead'
Leaf AC: 'passes.py remedy row deleted from docs/architecture.md offenders table' — the row is deleted, not corrected to reflect what is actually moved

G7 conflict rule: 'if you believe the parent is genuinely wrong, do NOT silently diverge the leaf — instead update the parent first (which stales the parent's own plan-review attestation and forces its re-review), and only then re-review the leaf against the corrected parent'
findings.finding: The leaf contradicts the parent's specified seam and resolves the discrepancy by deleting the parent row in the same change, rather than updating the parent spec first. The docs/architecture.md offenders row (the governing parent contract) specifies moving `pass4_coach` as part of the coach cluster into `coach_moves.py`; the leaf explicitly excludes `pass4_coach` and instead moves `triage_advisories` + `APPLY_NOW_MARGIN`, which the parent row does not mention at all. Per G7's conflict rule, the correct path is to update/correct the parent spec first (which forces its re-review), then re-review the leaf as a subset of the corrected parent — not to delete the stale row and implement the divergent split atomically in one change.
findings.location: Leaf plan section 'Deviation from the docs/architecture.md offenders row'; docs/architecture.md ~line 271 (the passes.py offenders-table row); leaf AC 'passes.py remedy row deleted from docs/architecture.md offenders table'
scenarios: An implementer reviewing the leaf plan against the parent spec sees the parent says 'move pass4_coach' but the leaf says 'pass4_coach stays' — this is a direct parent-child contradiction that G7 requires be resolved by updating the parent first

A future reviewer of the architecture.md (after this ticket lands) sees no remedy row for passes.py and no record that pass4_coach was considered and deliberately excluded from the move — the deletion removes that audit trail
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the leaf's AC 'passes.py remedy row deleted from docs/architecture.md offenders table' with: 'passes.py remedy row in docs/architecture.md is UPDATED to reflect the actual seam: `MOVE_REGISTRY`, `load_move_registry`, `triage_advisories`, `APPLY_NOW_MARGIN` → `coach_moves.py`; `pass4_coach` stays in `passes.py` (circular import — noted inline). Row is then removed once the file graduates off the allowlist via the separate graduation AC.' This makes the parent correction explicit and auditable rather than a silent deletion.

findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

findings.finding: The plan asserts coach_moves.py will import 'ONLY rebar.llm.review_kernel', but load_move_registry (the function being moved) also uses stdlib json and pathlib.Path — both currently imported in passes.py precisely because load_move_registry needs them.
findings.location: MOVE section: 'The new leaf imports ONLY rebar.llm.review_kernel (for validate_move_registry + DEFAULT_BLOCK_THRESHOLD) — no back-reference to passes.py, so no cycle.'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the import description to: 'The new leaf imports rebar.llm.review_kernel (for validate_move_registry + DEFAULT_BLOCK_THRESHOLD) plus stdlib json and pathlib — no back-reference to passes.py, so no cycle.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the comment at orchestrator.py:84–86 to reflect that MOVE_REGISTRY and load_move_registry now live in `coach_moves.py` (re-exported through passes.py), not directly in passes.py.
evidence: orchestrator.py:84: '# The Pass-4 move registry + loader live with their consumer (pass4_coach) in'
orchestrator.py:85: '# :mod:`.passes`; re-exported here for the historical ``orchestrator.MOVE_REGISTRY`` /'
Plan enumerates orchestrator.py:87 as an edit target for the re-import line, but does not mention the comment at line 84.
After the move, MOVE_REGISTRY and load_move_registry live in coach_moves.py; the comment's claim that they live 'in :mod:`.passes`' will be incorrect.
findings.finding: orchestrator.py line 84's comment — 'The Pass-4 move registry + loader live with their consumer (pass4_coach) in :mod:`.passes`' — becomes stale after the move: the registry and loader will live in coach_moves.py, not passes.py, yet the plan does not list updating this comment as an edit target.
scenarios: A developer reading orchestrator.py after the refactor is misled into looking in passes.py for the registry definitions rather than coach_moves.py.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the comment to: '# The Pass-4 move registry + loader live in :mod:`.coach_moves` (re-exported via :mod:`.passes`); re-exported here for the historical ``orchestrator.MOVE_REGISTRY`` / ``orchestrator.load_move_registry`` call sites.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
